formulas.name: Law of Demand
formulas.formula: As the price of a commodity increases, the quantity demanded decreases, ceteris paribus
formulas.notes: This law states that there is an inverse relationship between the price of a commodity and the quantity demanded of it.

formulas.name: Diminishing Marginal Utility (DMU) Law
formulas.formula: As the quantity of a commodity consumed increases, the marginal utility derived from each additional unit decreases, ceteris paribus
formulas.notes: This law explains the concept of decreasing satisfaction or pleasure derived from the consumption of a commodity.

formulas.name: Giffen's Paradox
formulas.formula: In a situation where the income effect dominates the substitution effect, a rise in price may lead to an increase in quantity demanded
formulas.notes: This paradox occurs when the income effect of a price change leads to an increase in the quantity demanded of a commodity.

formulas.name: Law of Diminishing Marginal Utility
formulas.formula: As the quantity of a commodity consumed increases, the marginal utility derived from each additional unit decreases
formulas.notes: This law explains the concept of decreasing satisfaction or pleasure derived from the consumption of a commodity.

formulas.name: Consumer's Equilibrium
formulas.formula: The point at which the consumer's budget line intersects the indifference curve, representing the optimal combination of two goods
formulas.notes: This concept represents the point at which the consumer is satisfied with their consumption of two goods.

formulas.name: Giffen Goods
formulas.formula: Goods for which a rise in price leads to an increase in quantity demanded, due to the income effect dominating the substitution effect
formulas.notes: This concept explains the situation where a rise in price leads to an increase in quantity demanded of a commodity.
